title: Understanding molecular mechanisms of disease through spatial proteomics.
author_string: Pankow, Sandra, Martínez-Bartolomé, Salvador, Bamberger, Casimir, Yates, John R
abstract: Mammalian cells are organized into different compartments that separate and facilitate physiological processes by providing specialized local environments and allowing different, otherwise incompatible biological processes to be carried out simultaneously. Proteins are targeted to these subcellular locations where they fulfill specialized, compartment-specific functions. Spatial proteomics aims to localize and quantify proteins within subcellular structures.
